wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Easy MCP project overview section focused on introducing the framework's purpose, capabilities, and value proposition. Explain how Easy MCP transforms annotated Dart functions into Model Context Protocol servers with minimal boilerplate code. Document the core benefits including rapid development, automatic schema generation, dual transport support, and annotation-driven development workflow. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners unfamiliar with Model Context Protocol and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase such as 'MCP tools', 'transport modes', and 'code generation'. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases like AI agent integrations and server automation. Document the monorepo structure with two main packages and their relationships.

wiki_catalogs.name: @Mcp Annotation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the @Mcp annotation documentation focused on transport configuration and server generation setup. Document the McpTransport enum values (stdio, http) with their specific use cases and implementation details. Explain the transport parameter configuration for different MCP server modes, including JSON-RPC protocol setup for stdio transport and HTTP server configuration for http transport. Detail the generateJson parameter and its impact on schema generation. Provide comprehensive examples showing @Mcp annotation usage on classes, libraries, and methods. Document parameter validation rules, default values, and inheritance behavior. Include practical examples demonstrating transport-specific configurations and their effects on generated server code. Address common configuration patterns, best practices for transport selection, and troubleshooting transport-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: STDIO Transport
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for STDIO transport implementation focused on Easy MCP's terminal-based communication system. Document the JSON-RPC protocol implementation including request/response handling, streaming support, and stdin/stdout channel management. Explain the terminal communication setup process, including environment preparation, process spawning, and bidirectional data flow. Detail the generated server architecture showing how StdioTemplate creates MCPServerWithTools extending MCPServer with ToolsSupport. Cover the template generation process including tool registration, parameter extraction, and result serialization. Document configuration options for transport settings, error handling mechanisms, and logging strategies. Include practical examples showing stdio server execution, debugging techniques, and performance considerations. Address security implications of terminal-based communication and best practices for production deployment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Primitive Type Handling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for primitive type handling in Easy MCP's type system integration. Document the automatic mapping from Dart primitive types (String, int, double, bool) to JSON Schema validation rules. Explain null safety implementation through optional type detection and '?' suffix handling. Detail optional parameter support and how the system distinguishes between required and optional parameters. Include examples of type conversion logic showing how 'String?' becomes optional string validation versus 'String' becoming required string validation. Document the fallback behavior for unsupported primitive types and default schema generation. Explain integration with the broader schema building pipeline and how primitive types influence both server code generation and runtime validation. Address common scenarios like integer vs number distinction and boolean validation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Model Context Protocol Fundamentals
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Model Context Protocol fundamentals focused on explaining what MCP is and why it matters for AI agent development. Define the Model Context Protocol as a standardized communication interface between AI agents and external services. Explain the protocol's core concepts including contexts, requests, responses, and the bidirectional communication model. Document how MCP enables secure, structured communication between AI agents and various services through standardized JSON-RPC messages. Include practical examples of MCP use cases such as tool execution, resource access, and context management. Explain the benefits of MCP for AI agent integration including security isolation, standardized interfaces, and extensibility. Address the relationship between MCP and existing web protocols, highlighting unique advantages for AI agent scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: AST Analysis and Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AST analysis and processing subsystem. Document the integration with dart:analyzer for reliable code extraction and annotation processing. Explain the library scanning mechanism that identifies @Mcp and @Tool annotations throughout the codebase. Detail the annotation extraction process including parameter introspection, type analysis, and metadata collection. Cover the cross-library tool discovery system that scans imported libraries for tools while maintaining package boundaries. Document the type system integration including primitive type mapping, generic type handling, and custom class serialization. Include technical details about the AST traversal patterns, element filtering, and annotation validation. Address performance considerations for large codebases and provide debugging strategies for annotation processing issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the user management system example implementation. Document the UserStore class architecture, including persistent storage mechanisms using JSON files, caching strategies, and data loading/saving patterns. Explain the CRUD operations implemented through @Tool annotations, covering user creation, retrieval, listing, deletion, and searching functionality. Detail the cross-store relationships with TodoStore, including cascade deletion handling and foreign key reference cleanup. Document the data models, parameter validation, error handling patterns, and file I/O operations. Include practical examples of each tool method, explaining the annotation parameters, return types, and expected behaviors. Address performance considerations, cache invalidation strategies, and production-ready patterns demonstrated in the implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cross-Library Tool Discovery
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for cross-library tool discovery mechanism. Explain how the framework scans the current library and all package-local imports to aggregate tools from multiple sources. Document the import scanning algorithm that identifies tools annotated with @Tool across different libraries within the same package. Detail the alias derivation system that ensures unique naming for tools from imported libraries, including the automatic suffixing mechanism for duplicate aliases. Explain the package URI parsing logic that distinguishes between local package imports and external dependencies. Cover the sourceImport and sourceAlias metadata injection that tracks tool provenance. Include practical examples showing how to organize multi-package projects for optimal tool discovery, configuration options for controlling discovery scope, and troubleshooting common issues with import resolution and alias conflicts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for Easy MCP focused on rapid onboarding and initial setup. Document prerequisites including Dart SDK requirements and environment setup. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for adding dependencies to pubspec.yaml, including both easy_mcp_annotations and easy_mcp_generator packages. Include a complete quick start tutorial walking through creating the first MCP server, from dependency addition to server execution. Demonstrate the annotation syntax with practical examples showing @mcp and @tool decorators. Cover the build process using build_runner and explain the generated code structure. Include troubleshooting tips for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Make content accessible to developers new to both Easy MCP and Model Context Protocol while providing clear next steps for deeper exploration.

wiki_catalogs.name: @Tool Annotation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the @Tool annotation documentation focused on tool metadata definition and documentation enhancement. Document the description parameter for overriding method doc comments and providing tool descriptions. Explain the icons parameter for specifying icon URLs and visual representation options. Detail the execution parameter which is currently deprecated but reserved for future use. Provide comprehensive examples showing @Tool annotation usage with various parameter combinations. Document the relationship between @Tool and @Mcp annotations, including annotation precedence and inheritance rules. Include practical examples demonstrating tool documentation best practices, icon specification guidelines, and proper description formatting. Address the deprecation notice for execution parameter and future compatibility considerations. Explain how @Tool metadata integrates with the code generation pipeline to enhance tool discoverability and user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: HTTP Transport
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for HTTP transport implementation focused on Easy MCP's web-based communication system. Document the Shelf framework integration including HTTP server setup, request routing, and response handling. Explain the bidirectional streaming architecture using StreamController and StreamChannel for real-time communication between HTTP requests and MCP server. Detail the port configuration options, loopback interface binding, and server lifecycle management. Cover the template generation process showing how HttpTemplate creates HTTP endpoints that proxy requests to the MCP server. Document the response buffering mechanism using completer queues and stream synchronization. Include practical examples showing HTTP server startup, endpoint testing, and integration with web clients. Address security considerations including CORS handling, authentication options, and HTTPS deployment. Document monitoring approaches, error handling strategies, and performance optimization techniques for HTTP-based MCP servers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Complex Type Support
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for complex type support in Easy MCP's type system integration. Document generic type handling including List<T> collections and how item types are recursively processed. Explain custom class serialization approaches and object schema generation from Dart classes. Detail nested object structures and property mapping strategies. Document collection type support beyond lists including maps and other iterable types. Include examples of complex type scenarios such as List<User>, Map<String, Todo>, and nested object hierarchies. Address type introspection mechanisms used to extract schema information from Dart types. Explain the relationship between type information and JSON Schema generation for complex validation rules. Document limitations and unsupported type combinations with recommended workarounds. Cover integration with the AST analysis phase and how complex types influence both code generation and runtime behavior.

wiki_catalogs.name: Annotation System Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Easy MCP annotation system focused on the @Mcp and @Tool annotations that form the foundation of the framework. Explain how annotations serve as declarative markers that tell the code generator which functions to expose as MCP tools and how to configure transport settings. Document the @Mcp annotation parameters including transport selection (stdio/http), JSON generation options, and their impact on the generated code. Detail the @Tool annotation for tool metadata including description, icon URLs, and deprecation handling. Provide concrete examples showing proper annotation usage on classes, libraries, and methods. Explain the annotation processing workflow from source code to generated server implementations. Include best practices for annotation placement and parameter configuration. Address common annotation-related issues and troubleshooting approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the template system architecture and rendering engine. Document the template-based code generation approach including transport-specific template implementations for stdio and HTTP modes. Explain the template rendering process, import resolution mechanisms, and code generation strategies. Detail the template inheritance patterns and how transport configurations are applied during generation. Cover the template variable substitution system and how tool metadata is integrated into generated code. Document the template compilation process and error handling for malformed templates. Include examples of template customization and extension points for adding new transport modes. Address template caching strategies and performance optimization techniques for repeated generations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Task Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the task management system example implementation. Document the TodoStore class architecture, including persistent storage patterns, task lifecycle management, and complex data relationships. Explain the many-to-many relationship implementation between users and tasks, covering bidirectional associations and referential integrity maintenance. Detail the CRUD operations for tasks, including creation, assignment, completion tracking, and bulk operations. Document the data models with nested object structures, optional fields, and relationship mappings. Include practical examples of task management workflows, explaining tool method implementations, parameter handling, and return value patterns. Address complex scenarios like cross-store operations, data consistency, and performance optimization techniques used in the implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Type Serialization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for custom type serialization capabilities. Document the advanced type introspection system that handles complex Dart types including generics, custom classes, and nested structures. Explain the cycle detection mechanism that prevents infinite recursion during schema generation for self-referencing objects. Detail the JSON Schema generation process for custom classes, including property mapping, required field detection, and nullable type handling. Cover the import URI extraction for List inner types and custom class serialization. Document the template-based serialization system that generates proper JSON encoding/decoding code for complex types. Include examples of serializing nested objects, handling optional properties, managing bidirectional relationships, and optimizing serialization performance. Address common serialization challenges such as circular references, polymorphic types, and custom serialization logic implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Concepts
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Easy MCP core concepts focused on fundamental understanding of the framework's architecture and design principles. Explain Model Context Protocol basics and its role in AI agent communication. Document the annotation system including @mcp for transport configuration and @tool for tool metadata definition. Describe the code generation workflow from annotated functions to executable MCP servers. Explain the dual transport modes (stdio and HTTP) and their use cases. Cover the AST-based parsing approach using dart:analyzer and the template-driven code generation system. wiki_overview.content:
Now I have enough information to create a comprehensive analysis of this project. Let me compile everything into a structured blog post.

<blog>

# Easy MCP: A Dart Code Generation Framework for Model Context Protocol Servers

## Project Introduction

**Easy MCP** is a sophisticated Dart code generation framework that transforms annotated functions into fully functional Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. The project consists of a monorepo containing three interconnected packages that work together to provide a seamless developer experience for creating MCP-compliant tools.

### Purpose Statement
The framework addresses the complexity of building MCP servers by abstracting away the boilerplate code, allowing developers to focus on business logic while automatically generating transport-layer implementations, schema validation, and server infrastructure.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Simplify MCP Server Development**: Eliminate repetitive code writing for common server patterns
- **Type Safety**: Leverage Dart's strong typing system for automatic schema generation
- **Flexibility**: Support multiple transport mechanisms (stdio and HTTP)
- **Developer Experience**: Provide intuitive annotations and clear separation of concerns

### Target Audience
- Dart developers building AI agent integrations
- Teams requiring standardized tool interfaces for AI applications
- Developers working with the Model Context Protocol ecosystem

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modular architecture with clear separation between annotation definitions, code generation logic, and runtime server implementations.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[User Code] --> B[easy_mcp_annotations]
    B --> C[easy_mcp_generator]
    C --> D[Generated MCP Server]
    D --> E[dart_mcp Runtime]
    
    F[Build Process] --> G[Analyzer AST]
    G --> H[Code Generation]
    H --> I[Template Rendering]
    
    J[Example Project] --> K[HTTP Transport]
    J --> L[STDIO Transport]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Core Modules:**
- **Annotations Package**: Defines the `@Mcp` and `@Tool` decorators
- **Generator Package**: Processes annotations and generates server code
- **Example Package**: Demonstrates practical usage patterns

**Design Patterns:**
- **Template Method Pattern**: Used in transport-specific server generation
- **Builder Pattern**: Implemented in schema and code generation
- **Annotation Processing**: Leverages Dart's analyzer for AST traversal

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Dev as Developer
    participant Anno as Annotations
    participant Gen as Generator
    participant Template as Templates
    participant Server as Generated Server
    
    Dev->>+Anno: Add @Mcp/@Tool annotations
    Anno->>+Gen: Source code analysis
    Gen->>+Template: Extract metadata & types
    Template->>+Server: Generate transport-specific code
    Server-->>-Dev: Ready-to-run MCP server
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Core Annotation Definitions** (`packages/easy_mcp_annotations/lib/mcp_annotations.dart`)
- `@Mcp` decorator controlling transport mode and JSON generation
- `@Tool` decorator providing metadata and descriptions
- Enum-based transport selection (stdio/http)

**Code Generation Engine** (`packages/easy_mcp_generator/lib/builder/mcp_builder.dart`)
- AST-based analysis using Dart's analyzer package
- Cross-library import scanning for tool discovery
- Transport-specific code generation

**Template System** (`packages/easy_mcp_generator/lib/builder/templates.dart`)
- Separate templates for STDIO and HTTP transports
- Automatic schema generation from Dart types
- Dynamic import resolution for custom types

### Configuration Approach

**Workspace Management** (`pubspec.yaml`)
- Melos-based workspace management for multi-package coordination
- Shared build scripts and development workflows
- Consistent dependency management across packages

**Build Configuration** (`packages/easy_mcp_generator/lib/builder/mcp_builder.dart`)
- Extension-based file watching (`.dart` → `.mcp.dart`, `.mcp.json`)
- Conditional JSON metadata generation
- Transport-aware code generation

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies:**
- `analyzer`: AST processing and type introspection
- `source_gen`: Code generation framework foundation
- `dart_mcp`: Runtime server implementation
- `shelf`: HTTP server for HTTP transport mode

**Development Dependencies:**
- `build_runner`: Code generation orchestration
- `test`: Unit testing framework
- `melos`: Workspace management and automation

### Integration Points

**Transport Layer Integration:**
- STDIO transport using JSON-RPC protocol
- HTTP transport leveraging Shelf framework
- Bidirectional streaming for real-time communication

**Schema Integration:**
- Automatic JSON Schema generation from Dart types
- Support for complex nested structures
- Optional parameter handling and validation

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class Mcp {
        +McpTransport transport
        +bool generateJson
    }
    
    class Tool {
        +String description
        +List~String~ icons
    }
    
    class McpBuilder {
        +buildExtensions
        +build()
        -extractToolsFromLibrary()
        -generateJsonMetadata()
    }
    
    class StdioTemplate {
        +generate()
        -generateToolHandlers()
    }
    
    class HttpTemplate {
        +generate()
        -handleRequest()
    }
    
    McpBuilder --> Mcp : annotates
    McpBuilder --> Tool : discovers
    McpBuilder --> StdioTemplate : generates
    McpBuilder --> HttpTemplate : generates
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Annotation-Based Development:**
- Simple decorators transform existing Dart functions into MCP tools
- Automatic documentation extraction from doc comments
- Flexible parameter handling with type safety

**Multi-Transport Support:**
- STDIO transport for traditional terminal-based communication
- HTTP transport for modern web-based integration
- Consistent API across different transport mechanisms

**Advanced Type Handling:**
- Automatic schema generation from Dart type systems
- Support for generics, optionals, and complex nested structures
- Custom class serialization with JSON encoding

### Implementation Highlights

**AST-Based Analysis** (`packages/easy_mcp_generator/lib/builder/mcp_builder.dart`)
- Comprehensive library scanning including imports
- Type introspection for automatic schema generation
- Cross-package tool discovery and aggregation

**Template-Based Code Generation** (`packages/easy_mcp_generator/lib/builder/templates.dart`)
- Transport-specific template rendering
- Dynamic import resolution for custom types
- Automatic parameter extraction and conversion

**Schema Generation** (`packages/easy_mcp_generator/lib/builder/schema_builder.dart`)
- JSON Schema compliance from Dart types
- Recursive schema building for complex structures
- Required field detection and validation

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> AnnotationProcessing
    AnnotationProcessing --> ASTAnalysis
    ASTAnalysis --> SchemaGeneration
    SchemaGeneration --> CodeGeneration
    CodeGeneration --> TransportSelection
    TransportSelection --> ServerGeneration
    ServerGeneration --> [*]
    
    TransportSelection --> StdioServer : stdio
    TransportSelection --> HttpServer : http
```

**Development Workflow:**
- Annotation application in user code
- Build step triggers code generation
- Generated server compiles and runs
- Integration with MCP clients and agents

**Type System Integration:**
- Primitive type mapping to JSON Schema
- Generic type handling with recursion
- Custom class serialization support
- Optional parameter validation

---

**Sources:**
- [pubspec.yaml](pubspec.yaml)
- [mcp_annotations.dart](packages/easy_mcp_annotations/lib/mcp_annotations.dart)
- [mcp_generator.dart](packages/easy_mcp_generator/lib/mcp_generator.dart)
- [mcp_builder.dart](packages/easy_mcp_generator/lib/builder/mcp_builder.dart)
- [templates.dart](packages/easy_mcp_generator/lib/builder/templates.dart)
- [schema_builder.dart](packages/easy_mcp_generator/lib/builder/schema_builder.dart)
- [example.dart](example/bin/example.dart)
- [user_store.dart](example/lib/src/user_store.dart)
- [todo_store.dart](example/lib/src/todo_store.dart)

</blog>
